title: Infinix Note 60 Pro unboxing and hands-on
description: This is the freshly announced Infinix Note 60 Pro, and it looks a lot like an iPhone 17 Pro Max. With that out of the way, let's do an unboxing. The phone ships with a generous helping of accessories - USB cable, 90W charger, USB earbuds, and a MagSafe-enabled case. Let's talk specs. The Infinix Note 60 Pro uses the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 SoC with a vapor chamber for cooling. Infinix says to expect up to 120fps in popular titles like Honor of Kings. The phone's 6,500mAh battery (some markets will get 6,000mAh) has bypass charging for gaming, too. Speaking of charging, you get 90W...

title: Xiaomi 17 Ultra Leica Edition is headed to global markets with a different name
description: If you've been wondering whether the Xiaomi 17 Ultra Leica Edition (or Xiaomi 17 Ultra by Leica) is headed to international markets, we have some good news for you. The answer is yes, and this was recently confirmed by its certification in Thailand by NBTC. That has revealed that it's going to be offered under a different name though: it will be called Leica Leitzphone powered by Xiaomi. That's a mouthful but it kind of makes sense - there have been three previous Leitz Phones so far, all exclusive to Japan, and so Leica seems to have given up on that line and replaced it with its...

title: Google Pixel 10a video ad leaks mere hours before the official unveiling
description: Google is finally making the oft-leaked Pixel 10a fully official in a matter of hours, on February 18. Ahead of that, an ad that Google will run for the device in France has now been outed, you can take a look below. French Pixel 10a ad.[image or embed]— Roland Quandt (@rquandt.bsky.social) February 17, 2026 at 6:56 PM As you can see, AI photo features get the first shoutout, followed by "30 h" battery life, whatever that means, and the colors are shown as well. But the most interesting thing is that this ad mentions a reduced price for the device. This is probably meant to say that...

title: Infinix Note 60 and Note 60 Pro unveiled with vapor chambers, 6,000+ mAh batteries
description: Infinix is launching a new Note series with a vanilla and a Pro model. The Pro is the first Infinix phone to use a Snapdragon chip since 2019 and it features a vapor chamber to keep it cool. The Infinix Note 60 Pro is powered by the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4, a 4nm chip from last year. It is paired with 8/12GB of RAM and 256GB storage. The 3D IceCore vapor chamber (4,758mm²) and 0.36mm copper sheet work together to reduce temperature by up to 3°C during intense gaming. Infinix Note 60 Pro The Pro also features an Active Matrix Display – a dot-style display on the camera island that...

title: vivo X300 FE color options, RAM, and storage variants tipped
description: vivo is expected to launch the X300 FE soon. The phone has recently received certifications in multiple regions and was also spotted on Geekbench. Now, a tipster has revealed its color options, along with RAM and storage variants. According to a tipster, the upcoming vivo X300 FE will be offered in 12GB/256GB and 12GB/512GB configurations. The tipster adds that the phone will be available in Black and Blue, but expects there to be other color options. vivo S50 Pro mini The vivo X300 FE is expected to be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Gen 5 SoC. As per the recent Geekbench listing, the...

title: Infinix Smart 20 retailer listing confirms more specs and pricing
description: Last month, the Infinix Smart 20 was spotted in the Google Play Console, and that revealed some of its specs. Now, the phone has been listed as up for sale by Lazada in the Philippines, complete with pricing. It's unclear if this represents the phone's actual launch or if it's a premature listing - the latter seems a tiny bit more probable since there are some typos as well as some missing info. Anyway, the phone is priced at PHP 4,499 (approximately $77) with 4GB of RAM and 64GB of storage, and PHP 5,830 (about $100) if you want 128GB of storage (the RAM stays the same). The phone...

title: Google is developing face unlocking tech on par with iPhone Face ID for the Pixel 11 family
description: Google is rumored to be developing better face unlocking tech for both the Pixel 11 family and upcoming Chromebooks. This endeavor is internally codenamed Project Toscana. According to someone who allegedly tested it, it will work without any additional visible sensors on the Pixel phones. It's unclear if it's using infrared like iPhones do, but if it is, then the IR bits are hidden under the display (and Apple is rumored to be going the same route for the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max). So basically everything looks the same as on a Pixel 10, but, importantly, the face unlocking works as well...

title: Rumor: iPad Pro series won't get a major update anytime soon
description: According to a new rumor from China, Apple’s iPad Pro lineup may be left without a major update for years to come. Leaker Instant Digital believes the current M5 generation iPad Pro 13 and iPad Pro 11 are not going to get significant updates anytime soon. This rumor makes sense as Apple tends to wait a hefty amount of time before bringing any drastic changes to its Pro tablets. The iPad Pro received its first major redesign in 2018 with the Pro 11 (2018) and Pro 12.9 (2018) and then in 2024 with the M4 generation models. According to the new rumor, there are several factors at play,...

title: Samsung advertises the Galaxy S26 Ultra's low-light camera performance with an AI video
description: Samsung recently teased the upcoming Galaxy S26 Ultra's low-light photography chops, and today the company's arm in the US has released a short video teasing the phone's low-light video recording capabilities. That's nothing out of the ordinary, but here's the thing - the teaser video itself was "generated with the assistance of AI tools", as the company's disclaimer clearly notes at the bottom. That's... interesting, to put it nicely. Why wouldn't Samsung just share a video captured in low-light conditions with the Galaxy S26 Ultra, if the idea is to tease how good it is at...

title: Dreame shows off gilded Aurora phone with three designs that are steeped in symbolism
description: After smart rings and action cameras Dreame, a brand that got famous for its robotic vacuums, is expanding into the smartphone business. A couple of weeks ago at a launch event in Poland, the company showed off the Dreame RS1, Air1 and E1. Now the company’s Weibo account is posting images of a luxury Dreame Aurora that doesn’t seem to be based on any one of those three. The bejeweled, gold-covered design certainly screams “opulence”... or maybe it screams something else depending on your taste levels. There are three different designs to choose from, maybe you like one more than the...

title: Samsung announces European Galaxy Book6 series prices and release dates
description: Earlier this year, during CES, Samsung announced its Galaxy Book6 laptop series powered by Intel's Core Ultra Series 3 processors, but it didn't disclose prices and launch dates for the European market. So today, the company is finally revealing the prices of all configurations and their availability. Pre-orders kick off on February 25 next week, just in time for the Galaxy S series Unpacked event. Actual shipments will commence on March 11, and the first European markets to get the Galaxy Book6 series are Belgium, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, the Scandinavian countries,...

title: Motorola is allegedly working on a fourth FIFA World Cup 26 Edition phone
description: Motorola already launched the Razr 60 FIFA World Cup 26 Edition, and we recently heard that the company would also make FIFA World Cup 26 themed versions of both the Signature and the Razr Fold. Today the same source is back with another bombshell: Motorola is apparently working on a fourth World Cup special edition. There will also be the Motorola Edge 70 Fusion FIFA World Cup 26 Edition. Motorola Razr 60 FIFA World Cup 26 Edition Clearly then, Motorola is going all-in with its collaboration with FIFA for this year's World Cup, which is taking place in Canada, the US, and Mexico...

title: Exynos 2600 inside Galaxy S26 posts impressive Ray Tracing score
description: Samsung's Exynos 2600 SoC, which will power (at least) the base model Galaxy S26 models (in some regions, it remains to be seen), boasts the best Ray Tracing in the game. The Exynos 2600's graphics processor, the Xclipse 960, posted a chart-topping 8,321 points in Basemark's In Vitro 1.0's Ray Tracing test, beating out the Adreno 840 inside the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 by some 8%, and the Mali-G1-Ultra inside the MediaTek Dimensity 9500 by 17%. The Xclipse 960 uses AMD's latest RDNA 4 Ray Tracing technology, found in AMD's current-gen graphics cards. So if graphics are your...

title: Tecno Camon 50 and 50 Pro debut with Helio G200 chipset and 6,150mAh batteries
description: Tecno’s Camon 50 series was supposed to get unveiled at MWC next month, but the Camon 50 and Camon 50 Pro are already official in several markets in Africa. The pair share similar specs, but the Pro model does get a curved screen and a 3x telephoto camera. Tecno Camon 50 Pro (left) and Camon 50 (right) Camon 50 Pro is built around a curved 6.78" AMOLED with FHD+ resolution and a 144Hz refresh rate. The panel features a 32MP selfie camera and an under-display optical fingerprint scanner. The non-Pro model features the same screen specs, selfie cam and fingerprint scanner but opts...

title: Lava Bold N2 4G unveiled with a 5,000mAh battery, IP64 rating
description: Lava, the Indian smartphone brand, has launched the Bold N2 4G in the country. It is an affordable 4G handset available in two color options and costs less than $100. The Lava Bold N2 4G succeeds the Bold N1 4G, which was unveiled in June last year. While the new model shares many of its specs with its predecessor, it features a refreshed design and improved ingress protection. The phone is powered by an octa-core Unisoc SC9863A chipset, which is paired with 4GB of RAM and 64GB of internal storage. It does feature a microSD card slot for storage expansion. Lava has updated the...

title: Nothing confirms Phone (4a) series launch event
description: Nothing has been teasing its Phone (4a) series for a while now and we finally have a launch date to look forward to – March 5. Nothing CEO Carl Pei shared a poster that hijacks the event invite for Apple’s own March 4 special experience event with a bright pink spray-paint overlay. Nothing Phone (4a) series launch poster We’re expecting two phones from Nothing – the Phone (4a) and Phone (4a) Pro. The Phone (4a) series is rumored to be powered by the Snapdragon 7-series chipsets, with the vanilla model appearing in a Geekbench test run with the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 chip. Nothing...

title: Infinix GT 50 Pro design and key specifications leaked
description: Infinix is expected to launch the successor to the GT 30 Pro soon. While the brand has yet to share official details, the Infinix GT 50 Pro was spotted on Geekbench last month. Now, additional information, including design renders, has leaked. The leaked renders reveal a gaming-focused design for the GT 50 Pro, featuring a carbon-fiber weave pattern on the rear panel along with LED accents at the bottom. The device will reportedly sport a 6.78-inch AMOLED display with a 1.5K resolution and a refresh rate of 144Hz. It is said to be powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 8400 Ultimate SoC,...

title: Galaxy S26 Ultra's selfie camera tipped to use a new sensor
description: Samsung is set to finally unveil the Galaxy S26 series next week at the Galaxy Unpacked event in San Francisco. Just days before the announcement, a tipster has shared new details about the Galaxy S26 Ultra. According to well-known tipster Ice Universe, the Galaxy S26 Ultra will feature an upgraded selfie camera. While it will retain a 12MP front-facing camera, matching the Galaxy S25 Ultra, the device will use a new sensor. Galaxy S26 Ultra leaked press image The Galaxy S26 Ultra is tipped to switch to a Sony sensor, replacing the Samsung ISOCELL 3LU used in its predecessor. Other...

title: Oppo Find X10 series said to offer MagSafe-like wireless charging
description: Oppo’s Find X10 series is still several months away from launch, but a new leak has revealed details about the phones’ charging capabilities. The lineup is expected to include the Find X10, Find X10 Pro, and Find X10 Pro Max. According to a tipster on Weibo, the upcoming Find X10 lineup could include built-in magnets to support MagSafe-style wireless charging. Oppo Find X9 Pro Outside of Google’s Pixel 10 series, no other Android smartphones offer the feature. Even Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy S26 lineup is rumored to skip built-in magnets and support only standard Qi2 wireless...

title: Infinix Note 60 Ultra FCC certification reveals two battery capacities
description: The upcoming Infinix Note 60 Ultra, designed by famed Italian house Pininfarina, has been certified by the FCC with the model number X6877. The certification interestingly reveals that the phone will have two battery capacities - rated at 5,850 mAh and 6,840 mAh, and advertised as typical 6,000 mAh and 7,000 mAh, respectively. This leads us to believe that, like other brands, Infinix will offer the Note 60 Ultra with the lower capacity in some markets like Europe, and with the higher one elsewhere. The FCC has also revealed that the Note 60 Ultra will support 30W wireless...
